entries.name: Royal Botanic Garden Sydney
entries.desc: A harbourside botanical garden next to the Opera House, established in 1816 as one of Australia's oldest scientific institutions, with themed gardens and sweeping views of the harbour.

photo.alt: Garden path and lawns in the Royal Botanic Garden, Sydney
photo.credit: <!-- photo: royal-botanic-garden-sydney.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Garden in the Royal Botanic Gardens, Sydney.jpg" by Chris.sherlock2 · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Garden_in_the_Royal_Botanic_Gardens,_Sydney.jpg -->

entries.name: Royal Botanic Gardens Melbourne
entries.desc: A 19th-century botanical garden beside the Yarra River, laid out in a landscaped style around ornamental lakes, with themed beds and a large collection of plants from around the world.

photo.alt: View across the Royal Botanic Gardens, Melbourne
photo.credit: <!-- photo: royal-botanic-gardens-melbourne.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Royal Botanic Gardens View Melbourne.JPG" by Cookaa · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Royal_Botanic_Gardens_View_Melbourne.JPG -->

entries.name: Schönbrunn Palace Gardens
entries.desc: The Baroque gardens of the former Habsburg summer residence, with symmetrical parterres rising to the Gloriette hilltop pavilion, plus a maze and one of the world's oldest zoos.

photo.alt: The Gloriette pavilion above the gardens of Schönbrunn Palace, Vienna
photo.credit: <!-- photo: schonbrunn-gloriette.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Gloriette, Schlosspark Schönbrunn, Wien.jpg" by Peter Ettel · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Gloriette,_Schlosspark_Sch%C3%B6nbrunn,_Wien.jpg -->

entries.name: Jardim Botânico
entries.desc: Rio's botanical garden, founded in the early 19th century, noted for its long avenue of towering royal palms, orchid houses, and collections of Brazilian and tropical plants.

photo.alt: Arboretum grounds of the Jardim Botânico, Rio de Janeiro
photo.credit: <!-- photo: jardim-botanico-rio.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Arboreto do Jardim Botânico, Rio de Janeiro.jpg" by Tania Freire · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Arboreto_do_Jardim_Bot%C3%A2nico,_Rio_de_Janeiro.jpg -->

entries.name: Butchart Gardens
entries.desc: A display garden created in a former limestone quarry near Victoria, with a Sunken Garden, rose garden, and Japanese and Italian gardens; a designated National Historic Site of Canada.

photo.alt: The Sunken Garden at Butchart Gardens, Brentwood Bay
photo.credit: <!-- photo: butchart-sunken-gardens.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Butchart-SunkenGardens.JPG" by Mwoodbc · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Butchart-SunkenGardens.JPG -->

entries.name: Montreal Botanical Garden
entries.desc: One of the largest botanical gardens in the world, with themed outdoor gardens and greenhouses, notably its Chinese and Japanese gardens and a fall Gardens of Light lantern event.

photo.alt: Japanese Garden at the Montreal Botanical Garden
photo.credit: <!-- photo: montreal-botanical-garden-japanese.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Japanese Garden; Montreal Botanical Gardens, Montreal, Canada.jpg" by Alexan Ohan · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Japanese_Garden;_Montreal_Botanical_Gardens,_Montreal,_Canada.jpg -->

entries.name: Humble Administrator's Garden
entries.desc: The largest of Suzhou's classical gardens and a UNESCO site, a Ming-dynasty scholar's garden built around water, with linked ponds, pavilions, bridges, and covered walkways.

photo.alt: Ponds and pavilions at the Humble Administrator's Garden, Suzhou
photo.credit: <!-- photo: humble-administrators-garden-suzhou.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Humble Administrator's Garden in Suzhou, China (2015) - 17.JPG" by Another Believer · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Humble_Administrator%27s_Garden_in_Suzhou,_China_(2015)_-_17.JPG -->

entries.name: Yu Garden (Yuyuan)
entries.desc: A classical Chinese garden in Shanghai's old city dating from the Ming dynasty, packed with rockeries, halls, ponds, and a dragon-topped wall within a compact walled layout.

photo.alt: Huxinting Teahouse over the pond at Yu Garden, Shanghai
photo.credit: <!-- photo: yu-garden-huxinting-teahouse.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Huxinting Teahouse at the Yu Garden pond, Old City, Shanghai.jpg" by Peter K Burian · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Huxinting_Teahouse_at_the_Yu_Garden_pond,_Old_City,_Shanghai.jpg -->

entries.name: Jardín Botánico de Bogotá
entries.desc: Colombia's largest botanical garden, focused on high-altitude Andean and páramo ecosystems, with greenhouses and collections representing the country's varied plant life.

photo.alt: Jardín Botánico de Bogotá, Colombia's largest botanical garden
photo.credit: <!-- photo: jardin-botanico-bogota.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Jardin Botanico Bogota (4).JPG" by Javadumper · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Jardin_Botanico_Bogota_(4).JPG -->

entries.name: Gardens of Versailles
entries.desc: The formal gardens of the royal palace, laid out by André Le Nôtre in the 17th century as the definitive French formal garden, with geometric parterres, canals, and elaborate fountains.

photo.alt: Aerial view of the formal gardens and palace of Versailles
photo.credit: <!-- photo: versailles-gardens-aerial.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Vue aérienne du domaine de Versailles par ToucanWings - Creative Commons By Sa 3.0 - 073.jpg" by ToucanWings · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Vue_a%C3%A9rienne_du_domaine_de_Versailles_par_ToucanWings_-_Creative_Commons_By_Sa_3.0_-_073.jpg -->

entries.name: Monet's Garden
entries.desc: The home and garden of painter Claude Monet, comprising a flower garden and a Japanese-inspired water garden whose lily pond and green bridge inspired his Water Lilies series.

photo.alt: Claude Monet's garden and flower beds in Giverny, France
photo.credit: <!-- photo: monets-garden-giverny.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Giverny Claude Monet's Garden.JPG" by Sergey Prokopenko · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Giverny_Claude_Monet%27s_Garden.JPG -->

entries.name: Jardin des Plantes
entries.desc: France's main botanical garden, founded in the 17th century as a royal medicinal herb garden and now part of the National Museum of Natural History, with greenhouses and a small zoo.

photo.alt: Jardin des Plantes and the Muséum national d'Histoire naturelle in Paris
photo.credit: <!-- photo: jardin-des-plantes-paris.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Jardin des plantes et Muséum national d'histoire naturelle,Paris.JPG" by Amarena7 · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Jardin_des_plantes_et_Mus%C3%A9um_national_d%27histoire_naturelle,Paris.JPG -->

entries.name: Château de Villandry
entries.desc: A Renaissance château famous for its meticulously restored gardens arranged in geometric terraces, including an ornamental kitchen garden that patterns vegetables and herbs like a tapestry.

photo.alt: Aerial view of Château de Villandry and its geometric gardens
photo.credit: <!-- photo: chateau-de-villandry-gardens.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Aerial image of Château de Villandry (view from the southeast).jpg" by Carsten Steger · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Aerial_image_of_Ch%C3%A2teau_de_Villandry_(view_from_the_southeast).jpg -->

entries.name: Sanssouci Park
entries.desc: The extensive park surrounding Frederick the Great's Rococo summer palace, with terraced vineyards, fountains, and formal gardens dotted with palaces and pavilions.

photo.alt: Aerial view of Sanssouci Park and its terraced vineyard, Potsdam
photo.credit: <!-- photo: sanssouci-park-aerial.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC0 · "Sanssouci von oben, 2008.jpg" by noxoss (Bernd Petrikat) on Pixabay · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Sanssouci_von_oben,_2008.jpg -->

entries.name: Shalimar Bagh
entries.desc: A Mughal garden on the shore of Dal Lake, laid out in the early 17th century with terraces, a central water channel, and fountains framed by the Kashmir mountains.

photo.alt: Shalimar Bagh, the Mughal garden on Dal Lake, Srinagar
photo.credit: <!-- photo: shalimar-bagh-srinagar.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Shalimar Bagh, Srinagar.jpg" by Pradeepkjoshi · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Shalimar_Bagh,_Srinagar.jpg -->

entries.name: Bagh-e Fin
entries.desc: A classic Persian garden fed by a natural spring, laid out with symmetrical water channels, pools, and cypress-lined walkways; one of Iran's oldest surviving gardens and a UNESCO site.

photo.alt: Bagh-e Fin, the historic Persian garden in Kashan, Iran
photo.credit: <!-- photo: bagh-e-fin-kashan.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 2.5 · "Bagh-e-fin kashan.jpg" · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Bagh-e-fin_kashan.jpg -->

entries.name: Powerscourt Gardens
entries.desc: Formal terraced gardens on a grand 18th- and 19th-century estate in the Wicklow Mountains, blending Italianate terraces, ornamental lakes, and walled gardens against a mountain backdrop.

photo.alt: Terraced gardens and ornamental lake at Powerscourt Estate, Enniskerry
photo.credit: <!-- photo: powerscourt-gardens-enniskerry.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Enniskerry - Powerscourt Estate - 20130224151055.jpg" by Frabjousone · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Enniskerry_-_Powerscourt_Estate_-_20130224151055.jpg -->

entries.name: Villa d'Este
entries.desc: A UNESCO-listed Renaissance villa near Rome, celebrated for its terraced hillside garden of fountains, water cascades, and pools fed entirely by gravity, a model for later European gardens.

photo.alt: Water Organ Fountain and terraced hillside gardens at Villa d'Este, Tivoli
photo.credit: <!-- photo: villa-deste-tivoli-fountains.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Water Organ Fountain in Villa d'Este (Tivoli, Lazio).jpg" by Anna Eden 86 · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Water_Organ_Fountain_in_Villa_d%27Este_(Tivoli,_Lazio).jpg -->

entries.name: Boboli Gardens
entries.desc: Historic Medici gardens behind the Pitti Palace, an early example of the formal Italian garden with terraces, sculpture, grottoes, and avenues arranged across a Florentine hillside.

photo.alt: Amphitheatre and terraces of the Boboli Gardens behind Pitti Palace, Florence
photo.credit: <!-- photo: boboli-gardens-florence.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Jardín de Bóboli, Florencia, Italia, 2022-09-19, DD 26.jpg" by Diego Delso · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Jard%C3%ADn_de_B%C3%B3boli,_Florencia,_Italia,_2022-09-19,_DD_26.jpg -->

entries.name: Villa Rufolo & Villa Cimbrone
entries.desc: Two clifftop villa gardens high above the Amalfi Coast, offering panoramic sea views from terraces and belvederes; Villa Rufolo's grounds are said to have inspired Wagner.

photo.alt: Elevated view of Villa Rufolo's terraced flower gardens overlooking the Amalfi Coast in Ravello
photo.credit: <!-- photo: villa-rufolo-ravello.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Villa Rufolo Ravello 18.JPG" by Mentnafunangann · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Villa_Rufolo_Ravello_18.JPG -->

entries.name: Isola Bella Gardens
entries.desc: A Baroque terraced garden covering a small island on Lake Maggiore, built by the Borromeo family with tiered stone terraces, statuary, and resident white peacocks.

photo.alt: Terraced Baroque garden of Isola Bella on Lake Maggiore
photo.credit: <!-- photo: isola-bella-garden-lake-maggiore.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Isola Bella Garten.jpg" by Ingo Kuebler · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Isola_Bella_Garten.jpg -->

entries.name: Kenroku-en
entries.desc: Considered one of Japan's three great landscape gardens, a former feudal-lord garden with ponds, streams, teahouses, and winter rope supports that protect its pines from snow.

photo.alt: Kenroku-en landscape garden with pond and pines, Kanazawa
photo.credit: <!-- photo: kenrokuen-garden-kanazawa.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Kenrokuen Garden, Kanazawa (53621201294).jpg" by Winniepix · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Kenrokuen_Garden,_Kanazawa_(53621201294).jpg -->

meta: One of Japan's Three Great Gardens

entries.name: Shinjuku Gyoen
entries.desc: A large former imperial garden in central Tokyo combining Japanese landscape, formal French, and English gardens, and a popular spot for cherry-blossom viewing in spring.

photo.alt: Footbridge over a pond in Shinjuku Gyoen National Garden, Tokyo
photo.credit: <!-- photo: shinjuku-gyoen-tokyo.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Footbridge over a pond in Shinjuku Gyoen National Garden and NTT DoCoMo Yoyogi Building, Tokyo, Japan.jpg" by Basile Morin · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Footbridge_over_a_pond_in_Shinjuku_Gyoen_National_Garden_and_NTT_DoCoMo_Yoyogi_Building,_Tokyo,_Japan.jpg -->

entries.name: Ryōan-ji
entries.desc: A Zen temple famous for its dry rock garden, where fifteen stones sit on raked white gravel; the minimalist arrangement is among the best-known examples of Japanese Zen design.

photo.alt: Raked gravel and stones of the Ryōan-ji rock garden, Kyoto
photo.credit: <!-- photo: ryoanji-rock-garden-kyoto.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "RyoanJi-Dry garden.jpg" by Stephane D'Alu · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:RyoanJi-Dry_garden.jpg -->

entries.name: Korakuen
entries.desc: One of Japan's three great landscape gardens, an Edo-period strolling garden with broad lawns, ponds, and tea plantations, set across the river from Okayama Castle.

photo.alt: Korakuen garden lawns and pond with Okayama Castle across the river
photo.credit: <!-- photo: korakuen-garden-okayama-castle.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Korakuen (japanese garden) and Okayama castle.jpg" by Motokoka · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Korakuen_(japanese_garden)_and_Okayama_castle.jpg -->

meta: One of Japan's Three Great Gardens

entries.name: Adachi Museum of Art Garden
entries.desc: A meticulously maintained garden attached to an art museum, designed to be viewed like a living painting through the building's windows; repeatedly ranked among Japan's finest gardens.

photo.alt: Adachi Museum of Art garden designed to be viewed like a living painting
photo.credit: <!-- photo: adachi-museum-of-art-garden.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Adachi Museum of Art Garden 01.jpg" by Bernard Gagnon · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Adachi_Museum_of_Art_Garden_01.jpg -->

entries.name: Jardin Majorelle
entries.desc: A compact botanical garden created by painter Jacques Majorelle and later owned by Yves Saint Laurent, known for its cobalt-blue buildings amid cacti, bamboo, and palms.

photo.alt: Cobalt-blue villa in Jardin Majorelle amid cacti and palms
photo.credit: <!-- photo: jardin-majorelle-blue-villa.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 2.0 · "Blue villa in Majorelle garden (2845770484).jpg" by mwanasimba from La Réunion · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Blue_villa_in_Majorelle_garden_(2845770484).jpg -->

meta: Once Owned by Yves Saint Laurent

entries.name: Keukenhof
entries.desc: A spring-only flower garden in the bulb-growing region, planted each year with millions of tulips, daffodils, and hyacinths across landscaped grounds and themed pavilions.

photo.alt: Landscaped tulip gardens at Keukenhof
photo.credit: <!-- photo: keukenhof-tulip-gardens.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Keukenhof Tulip Gardens 1.JPG" by Targeman · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Keukenhof_Tulip_Gardens_1.JPG -->

entries.name: Peterhof Palace Gardens
entries.desc: An imperial palace and park on the Gulf of Finland, famed for its Grand Cascade and gilded fountains powered by a gravity-fed water system without pumps.

photo.alt: Grand Cascade gilded fountains at Peterhof Palace
photo.credit: <!-- photo: peterhof-grand-cascade.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Grand Cascade of Peterhof 01.jpg" by Florstein (Telegram:WikiPhoto.Space) · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Grand_Cascade_of_Peterhof_01.jpg -->

entries.name: Gardens by the Bay
entries.desc: A modern nature park by Marina Bay, known for its tree-like vertical gardens called Supertrees and two climate-controlled conservatories housing cloud-forest and dry-region plants.

photo.alt: Supertree Grove vertical gardens at Gardens by the Bay
photo.credit: <!-- photo: gardens-by-the-bay-supertree-grove.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Supertree Grove, Gardens by the Bay, Singapore - 20120704.jpg" by Khairul Nizam. · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Supertree_Grove,_Gardens_by_the_Bay,_Singapore_-_20120704.jpg -->

entries.name: Singapore Botanic Gardens
entries.desc: A UNESCO World Heritage tropical garden dating to the 1850s, with rainforest remnants, ornamental lakes, and a National Orchid Garden holding thousands of orchid varieties.

photo.alt: Symphony Lake at Singapore Botanic Gardens
photo.credit: <!-- photo: singapore-botanic-gardens-symphony-lake.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC0 · "Shaw Foundation Symphony Stage, Symphony Lake, Singapore Botanic Gardens - 20130210.jpg" by 玄史生 · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Shaw_Foundation_Symphony_Stage,_Symphony_Lake,_Singapore_Botanic_Gardens_-_20130210.jpg -->

entries.name: Kirstenbosch National Botanical Garden
entries.desc: A botanical garden on the eastern slopes of Table Mountain devoted to native South African flora, including fynbos and proteas, with a canopy walkway among the treetops.

photo.alt: Boomslang canopy walkway among the treetops at Kirstenbosch
photo.credit: <!-- photo: kirstenbosch-boomslang-canopy-walkway.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Boomslang Kirstenbosch 1.jpg" by Axxter99 · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Boomslang_Kirstenbosch_1.jpg -->

entries.name: Babylonstoren
entries.desc: A working farm and garden on a historic Cape Dutch estate in the Winelands, centred on a large formal fruit-and-vegetable garden where nearly everything grown is edible.

photo.alt: Historic Cape Dutch farm buildings and garden rows at Babylonstoren
photo.credit: <!-- photo: babylonstoren-estate.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Babylonstoren1.jpg" by Nina R from Africa · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Babylonstoren1.jpg -->

entries.name: Generalife Gardens
entries.desc: The garden estate of the Nasrid sultans beside the Alhambra, an emblematic Moorish garden of courtyards, channels, and fountains where water and greenery define the design.

photo.alt: Patio de la Acequia water channel and fountains at the Generalife
photo.credit: <!-- photo: generalife-patio-de-la-acequia.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC0 · "Patio de la Acequia fountains Generalife Granada Spain.jpg" by Jebulon · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Patio_de_la_Acequia_fountains_Generalife_Granada_Spain.jpg -->

entries.name: Real Alcázar Gardens
entries.desc: The gardens of Seville's royal palace, layered over centuries of Moorish and later Spanish design, with tiled courtyards, pavilions, pools, and orange trees behind the Alcázar walls.

photo.alt: Garden path at the Marchena Door in the Real Alcázar gardens
photo.credit: <!-- photo: real-alcazar-gardens-seville.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 2.0 · "Real Alcázar - Seville - gardens - Jardines del Marqués de la Vega-Inclán - Marchena Door (14739440241).jpg" by Elliott Brown · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Real_Alc%C3%A1zar_-_Seville_-_gardens_-_Jardines_del_Marqu%C3%A9s_de_la_Vega-Incl%C3%A1n_-_Marchena_Door_(14739440241).jpg -->

entries.name: Nong Nooch Tropical Garden
entries.desc: A large tropical botanical park near Pattaya combining themed formal gardens, one of the world's major cycad and palm collections, and cultural shows and elephant displays.

photo.alt: Formal French-style garden section at Nong Nooch Tropical Garden
photo.credit: <!-- photo: nong-nooch-french-garden.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Nong Nooch French garden 1.JPG" by Aleksasfi · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Nong_Nooch_French_garden_1.JPG -->

entries.name: Royal Botanic Gardens, Kew
entries.desc: A UNESCO World Heritage botanical research institution on the Thames, holding one of the world's largest plant collections, Victorian glasshouses, and a treetop walkway.

photo.alt: The Victorian Palm House glasshouse at Kew Gardens
photo.credit: <!-- photo: kew-gardens-palm-house.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Kew Gardens Palm House, London - July 2009.jpg" by Diliff · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Kew_Gardens_Palm_House,_London_-_July_2009.jpg -->

entries.name: Eden Project
entries.desc: A visitor attraction built in a reclaimed clay pit, with giant geodesic domes housing rainforest and Mediterranean biomes that recreate distinct global climates and plant communities.

photo.alt: The geodesic biome domes of the Eden Project in a reclaimed clay pit
photo.credit: <!-- photo: eden-project-biomes.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "The Eden Project, Cornwall.JPG" by JuliesNotebook · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:The_Eden_Project,_Cornwall.JPG -->

entries.name: Lost Gardens of Heligan
entries.desc: A historic estate garden that fell into neglect after the First World War and was restored in the 1990s, known for its productive kitchen gardens and subtropical jungle valley.

photo.alt: Productive walled kitchen garden at the Lost Gardens of Heligan
photo.credit: <!-- photo: heligan-kitchen-garden.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Lost Gardens of Heligan Kitchen Garden.JPG" by Northmetpit · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Lost_Gardens_of_Heligan_Kitchen_Garden.JPG -->

entries.name: Alnwick Garden
entries.desc: A contemporary formal garden beside Alnwick Castle, centred on a large cascade fountain and known for its enclosed Poison Garden of toxic and dangerous plants.

photo.alt: The Grand Cascade water feature at Alnwick Garden
photo.credit: <!-- photo: alnwick-garden-cascade.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Alnwick Garden Water Features.jpg" by MiloDenn · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Alnwick_Garden_Water_Features.jpg -->

entries.name: Sissinghurst Castle Garden
entries.desc: An influential English garden created around an Elizabethan tower by Vita Sackville-West and Harold Nicolson, designed as a series of themed garden rooms including its celebrated White Garden.

photo.alt: Aerial view of Sissinghurst Castle's tower and garden rooms
photo.credit: <!-- photo: sissinghurst-castle-garden.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Sissinghurst Castle Garden aerial view.jpg" by Lieven Smits · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Sissinghurst_Castle_Garden_aerial_view.jpg -->

entries.name: Stourhead
entries.desc: A landscape garden laid out in the 18th century around a lake, with classical temples, grottoes, and a circular walk regarded as a landmark of English picturesque design.

photo.alt: Stourhead landscape garden viewed from above, with its lake and classical temples
photo.credit: <!-- photo: stourhead-garden.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Stourhead Garden View from Above.JPG" by Daderot · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Stourhead_Garden_View_from_Above.JPG -->

entries.name: Royal Botanic Garden Edinburgh
entries.desc: One of the oldest botanical gardens in Britain, a leading centre for plant science with extensive glasshouses, a noted rock garden, and a large collection of Himalayan and Chinese plants.

photo.alt: The Palm House glasshouse at the Royal Botanic Garden Edinburgh
photo.credit: <!-- photo: royal-botanic-garden-edinburgh-palm-house.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Palm House, Royal Botanic Garden Edinburgh.JPG" by Ham · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Palm_House,_Royal_Botanic_Garden_Edinburgh.JPG -->

entries.name: Longwood Gardens
entries.desc: A former du Pont estate that is one of America's premier display gardens, known for its grand conservatory, illuminated fountain performances, and elaborate seasonal flower shows.

photo.alt: The grand conservatory at Longwood Gardens
photo.credit: <!-- photo: longwood-gardens-conservatory.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC0 · "Conservatory - Longwood Gardens - DSC01094.JPG" by Daderot · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Conservatory_-_Longwood_Gardens_-_DSC01094.JPG -->

entries.name: Huntington Library & Botanical Gardens
entries.desc: A research library, art collection, and botanical garden near Los Angeles, with themed grounds including a large desert garden and classical Japanese and Chinese gardens.

photo.alt: Wooden moon bridge over the koi pond in the Japanese Garden at the Huntington Library and Botanical Gardens
photo.credit: <!-- photo: huntington-japanese-garden-moon-bridge.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "180516 151019 Huntington Botanical Gdn - Japanese Garden, moon bridge" · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:180516_151019_Huntington_Botanical_Gdn_-_Japanese_Garden,_moon_bridge,_built_in_1912_by_craftsman_Toichiro_Kawai,_it_was_painted_bright_red_for_many_years.jpg -->

entries.name: Brooklyn Botanic Garden
entries.desc: An urban botanical garden in Brooklyn, known for its Japanese Hill-and-Pond Garden, cherry esplanade, and a spring cherry-blossom festival, alongside rose and native-flora gardens.

photo.alt: The Japanese Hill-and-Pond Garden at Brooklyn Botanic Garden
photo.credit: <!-- photo: brooklyn-botanic-garden-japanese-pond.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Brooklyn Botanic Garden - 20200911 - 18 - Japanese Hill-and-Pond Garden.jpg" by Andre Carrotflower · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Brooklyn_Botanic_Garden_-_20200911_-_18_-_Japanese_Hill-and-Pond_Garden.jpg -->

entries.name: Portland Japanese Garden
entries.desc: A hillside Japanese garden widely regarded as one of the most authentic outside Japan, combining several traditional garden styles among the trees of Washington Park.

photo.alt: The zig-zag bridge at Portland Japanese Garden in Washington Park
photo.credit: <!-- photo: portland-japanese-garden-zigzag-bridge.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC0 · "Zig-zag Bridge - Portland Japanese Garden - Portland, Oregon - DSC08315.jpg" by Daderot · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Zig-zag_Bridge_-_Portland_Japanese_Garden_-_Portland,_Oregon_-_DSC08315.jpg -->

entries.name: Chicago Botanic Garden
entries.desc: A botanical garden north of Chicago spread across a series of islands and lagoons, with more than two dozen themed display gardens including a Japanese garden and an English walled garden.

photo.alt: The Zig Zag Bridge connecting islands at Chicago Botanic Garden
photo.credit: <!-- photo: chicago-botanic-garden-zigzag-bridge.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 2.5 · "Chicago Botanic Garden - Zig Zag Bridge.jpg" by Kaosfere · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Chicago_Botanic_Garden_-_Zig_Zag_Bridge.jpg -->
